title: Tenant Readiness Education Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Tenant Readiness Education Programs 7 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CASCADE AIDS PROJECT CAP HOUSING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management to help individuals find housing and related resources. Services include short- and long-term housing search support and one-time emergency rental assistance for security deposits or eviction prevention. 520 NW Davis Street Suite 215 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Email (503) 223-5907 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY IN ACTION READY TO RENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 4-6-week course that covers key information for being a responsible and stable tenant. Participants work closely with housing specialists and peers to address the following: -Overcoming barriers to rental housing -Learning about your responsibilities as a tenant -Understanding your lease/rental agreement -Learning about landlord/tenant law -Taking steps towards repairing credit and establishing a budget -Getting referrals to landlords who consider Ready to Rent graduates Upon completion, participants receive a certificate that can be presented to landlords when applying for housing. 915 SW 3rd Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: For people with poor or no credit, past evictions, no rental history, criminal history, and/or other screening barriers to rental housing and who are low-income, at risk of homelessness, or currently homeless. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 889-9555 136 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF LINCOLN COUNTY COMMUNITY CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers life skills, parenting, financial literacy, nutrition, and second chance renters' classes. Classes are primarily for families utilizing shelter services, but may be opened to the general public. 5030 SE Highway 101 Lincoln City, OR 97367 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Classes: Varies Email (541) 614-0964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF YAMHILL COUNTY READY TO RENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an 18 hour class geared towards those who are working to overcome a bad rental history. The class covers key information for being a responsible and stable tenant. Participants explore screening barriers they may have and take steps to address those barriers. Upon completion, participants receive a certificate that can be presented to landlords when applying for housing. Certificates are valid for 18 months. Certificates can be used anywhere in Oregon. Landlords in Yamhill County are eligible to register for the Landlord Assurance Program which may cover damages, unpaid rent, or eviction costs if a Ready to Rent tenant vacates the unit, not in good standing, within 12 months of the move-in date. 135 NE Dunn Place Mcminnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Program is for people with poor/no credit, past evictions, no rental history, criminal history, and/or other screening barriers to rental housing and those who are low-income, at risk of homelessness, or currently homeless Hours: Varies Email (503) 883-4308 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH HOUSING AUTHORITY HOMEPATH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial coaching services with information about identifying short and long term financial goals, set spending plans, Individual Development Accounts (IDA), and budget management. Also provides a First Time Home Buyer Program which can help answer questions about the the home buying process, first time home buyer loan programs, building credit scores, and resolving challenges that may prevent homeownership. Also provides a series of workshops around the topics of financial fitness, home buying, Ready to Rent, foreclosure prevention, and default counseling. 1445 Avalon Street Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 884-0649 121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RENT WELL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 15-hour tenant education course. The course is divided into 12 sections and can be taken in-person, on Zoom, or as a self-paced eCourse. The course covers fair housing laws, rental screening process, eviction process, credit repair and building, building a housing portfolio, how to maintain a healthy home, and how to address a previous eviction, criminal history, or other barriers. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Classes: Varies Email (503) 515-1328 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY SECOND CHANCE RENTERS EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a series of eight classes designed to lay the foundation of what people with poor rental and/or credit history need to know in order to become successful and responsible tenants. Upon graduation and completion of all required activities, Participants receive a certificate of completion and performance guarantee that is honored by landlords throughout Lane County. 2890 Chad Drive Eugene, OR 97408 Eligibility: Individuals with barriers to renting (eviction history, poor credit history, ex-offender status, homelessness, etc.) Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, closed holidays Classes: Varies; most classes start at 5pm Email (541) 731-4857 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT CAP HOUSING SERVICES COMMUNITY IN ACTION READY TO RENT FAMILY PROMISE OF LINCOLN COUNTY COMMUNITY CLASSES HOUSING AUTHORITY OF YAMHILL COUNTY READY TO RENT KLAMATH HOUSING AUTHORITY HOMEPATH PROGRAM ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY SECOND CHANCE RENTERS EDUCATION
